name: Northern Sea Route / Arctic Shipping Corridor
climate: Arctic and subarctic; extreme cold, polar night in winter, midnight sun in summer; rapidly warming due to climate change

neighbors.name: Nordic Council
neighbors.relevance: Norway, Denmark, Finland, Sweden, Iceland are Arctic states and Nordic Council members; shared Arctic governance

neighbors.name: Five Eyes
neighbors.relevance: USA and Canada have Arctic sovereignty; intelligence monitoring of Russian Northern Sea Route activity

neighbors.name: Baltic Sea
neighbors.relevance: NSR western terminus connects to Baltic ports; Murmansk and Arkhangelsk feed Baltic shipping

neighbors.name: CIS
neighbors.relevance: Russia controls the Northern Sea Route; CIS logistics networks feed Arctic port infrastructure

neighbors.name: Belt and Road Initiative
neighbors.relevance: China declared a Polar Silk Road; investments in Yamal LNG and Arctic shipping infrastructure

links.description: NSR offers shorter Asia-Europe route bypassing Suez; competitive alternative as ice recedes
Arctic route avoids Malacca chokepoint for Asia-Europe trade; reduces transit time by 10-15 days
Nordic nations manage western Arctic access; Norwegian ports serve as NSR staging points
China's Polar Silk Road integrates Arctic shipping into BRI; Yamal LNG as anchor project
US and Canada contest Russian NSR transit fees and sovereignty claims; Northwest Passage disputes
Russia and China coordinate Arctic resource development and shipping through SCO framework
NSR cargo connects to Baltic ports; Murmansk-to-Rotterdam route as key western link
